compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P3 (Pothos) is present but in advanced senescence. P1 (String of Nickels) is present. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not clearly identifiable as distinct healthy specimens; the soil in the lower pot shows only a single, struggling leaf structure.

inventory_reconciliation.p4: Systemic Loss (No viable silver guest detected)

plant_audit.p1: String of Nickels: Stable, low-light growth, consistent color.
plant_audit.p3: Pothos: Significant chlorosis and necrosis. The large leaf is transitioning from yellow to brown/crisp. The rabbit anchor remains stable.
plant_audit.p4_lower_pot: Single leaf remnant remaining; high probability of total loss.

biome_observations.surface_debris: White perlite/grit present. No fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t3: Minimal change; P3 leaf yellowing initiated.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: P3 leaf necrosis accelerates; color shift from yellow to brown.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: P3 leaf tissue degradation continues; structural collapse of the leaf blade.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: P3 leaf is now largely necrotic/shriveled. The lower pot specimen shows no signs of recovery.

vision_report.anomalies: None detected; the decline appears to be a standard physiological failure due to environmental stress or root-zone issues.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of terminal decline for the P3 and P4 specimens. The Pothos (P3) is undergoing rapid senescence, likely due to root rot or light deficiency, as evidenced by the progressive browning and loss of turgor. The String of Nickels (P1) remains the most resilient occupant. The lower pot is effectively barren, with only a single, non-thriving leaf remaining.
